title: Voice complaint

presenting_history.text: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped voice-change, hoarseness, loss-of-voice, cough or URTI, reflux context, voice use, tobacco, duration, voice observation, and terminology fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

red_flags.text: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped persistent symptoms, respiratory distress, neck mass, smoking, laryngeal or neck assessment, referral, and worsening fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

exact_section.heading: Key action statements: assessment, escalation, and laryngoscopy

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented voice-care education, management discussion, questions, follow-up, and outcome review.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

source_limitations: Throat discomfort, sore throat, dysphagia, haemoptysis, ear pain, broad throat or ear examination, prior-record review, fixed two-week timing, and empirical reflux-management wording remain unsupported.
Laryngitis remains clinician-stated; isolated dysphonia must not trigger empirical reflux, antibiotic, or steroid treatment.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
